title: See Capture Convergence PASS: all S_9 states are instantly captured into periodic orbits
_fixture_purpose: Maps T.J.J. See's capture theory of cosmical evolution to QA orbit convergence on S_9. All 81 states are already on their periodic orbit (tau=0 for internal states). Extended initial conditions (outside {1..9}) have tau=1 (single mod-reduction = capture event). The resisting medium is maximally efficient: modular arithmetic captures instantly.
source_attribution: T.J.J. See, 'Researches on the Evolution of the Stellar Systems, Vol II: The Capture Theory of Cosmical Evolution' (1910); 'The Capture Theory of Satellites', PASP 21:127 (1909); 'Dynamical Theory of the Capture of Satellites and of the Division of Nebulae Under the Secular Action of a Resisting Medium'. QA mapping: Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-07.

see_capture_theory.statement: Bodies approaching a gravitational center through a resisting medium lose energy, their orbits circularize, and they are captured into stable periodic motion. The rate of capture depends on the strength of the resisting medium.
see_capture_theory.key_prediction: Stronger dissipation -> faster capture -> lower eccentricity at equilibrium.
see_capture_theory.application: Explains formation of satellites (Moon), binary stars, and planetary system architecture.

convergence.interpretation: All 81 internal S_9 states are already on their periodic orbit. QA modular arithmetic is a maximally strong resisting medium: capture is instantaneous. This is the limiting case of See's prediction — infinite medium strength implies zero transient time.

cosmos.see_analogue: Planets in wide orbits — largest capture cross-section, most probable outcome

satellite.see_analogue: Moons in close orbits — bounded oscillation, returned to primary

singularity.see_analogue: Direct collision — body falls into center, zero orbital motion

extended_initial_conditions.description: For (b_0, e_0) outside {1,...,9}, the mod-reduction (b_0-1)%9+1 is the capture event (tau=1). This models See's approach from infinity: the body is 'outside' the modular space, and the first mod-reduction brings it inside.

resisting_medium_mapping.see_concept: Physical resisting medium (gas, dust, aether) that dissipates orbital energy and circularizes eccentric orbits over time
resisting_medium_mapping.qa_concept: Modular reduction operator (% m) that projects arbitrary integers into {1,...,m}. The modulus m controls the 'strength' of the medium: smaller m = fewer states = stronger confinement = faster capture.

correspondence.qa: Distance from nearest orbit attractor in state space
Transient length tau before periodic orbit entry
L_3 operator required to leave S_m (modulus change, per [191])

witnesses.note: State (1,1) is on the 24-cycle cosmos orbit. Captured instantly. See analogue: a planet already in a stable wide orbit — no further dissipation needed.
State (3,3) is on the 8-cycle satellite orbit. v_3(f(3,3)) = v_3(9) = 2. See analogue: a moon in a bounded, close orbit.
State (9,9) is the fixed point. v_3(f(9,9)) = v_3(729) = 6. See analogue: direct radial infall — zero angular momentum, body reaches center.

Extended initial condition outside S_9. Mod-reduction: (15-1)%9+1=6, (22-1)%9+1=4 -> captured as (6,4) on cosmos orbit. tau=1 = single capture event. See analogue: body approaching from outside the system, captured on first pass through the resisting medium.
